source: Greenbarz · Curated from General Index (29-Apr-2026 settle)
note: Real GX assessments. Crude markets in steep backwardation following geopolitical risk repricing — see context fields. Brent CFD W1 at +9.74, BFOE M1/M3 spread +14.68. Refresh quarterly during launch.

grades.name: Dated Brent FOB NWE
grades.role: Global benchmark · ~70% of seaborne crude pricing
grades.context: Dated Brent ripped +6.5% week-on-week as geopolitical risk premium repriced. Curve in steep backwardation: M1 at $123, M3 at $104, 24-month forward at $72 — market pricing near-term tightness while expecting normalization. Brent CFDs printed +$9.74 (W1), confirming acute physical tightness in the prompt window.

grades.name: Oseberg
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Norwegian medium sweet
grades.context: Equinor-operated, eligible for delivery into Dated Brent. ~37° API. Trading $5.92/bbl over Dated Brent — refiners paying up for medium-sweet quality during the spike. Sits between Ekofisk and Troll in the basket pecking order.

grades.name: Ekofisk
grades.context: ConocoPhillips-operated, ~38° API, delivered Teesside. Light sweet quality preferred by European gasoline-skewed refiners. Currently $3.89/bbl over Dated Brent. The Ekofisk vs Johan Sverdrup spread is +$4.99 — a clean signal of premium-vs-sour bifurcation in the basket.

grades.name: Troll
grades.context: Equinor-operated, ~36° API, delivered Mongstad. The richest BFOET grade — highest distillate yield, currently the most expensive at $6.62/bbl over Dated Brent. When refining margins are wide, Troll commands its biggest premium. Production ~330 kb/d.

grades.name: Johan Sverdrup
grades.role: BFOET basket grade · Medium heavy sour
grades.context: Norway's largest field, ~28° API, ~0.8% sulfur. Heavier and more sour than the rest of BFOET — currently $1.10/bbl BELOW Dated Brent (basket floor). When Johan Sverdrup sets the Dated Brent price (cheapest deliverable), it signals medium-sour glut globally. Production ~720 kb/d, the basket's largest.

grades.name: Dubai Partial
grades.role: Asian benchmark · prices ~25 mb/d Mideast→Asia flows
grades.context: Brent-Dubai EFS at +$15.48 — wide spread means Asian refiners shifting hard to Mideast crude over Atlantic Basin barrels. Dubai M1/M3 spread at +$7.19 (steep backwardation, mirrors Brent). 21 partials traded in latest session at $106.50/bbl offer.

grades.name: IFAD Murban
grades.role: Light sour Mideast benchmark · ICE Murban futures
grades.context: ADNOC's flagship, ~40° API, ~0.8% sulfur. M1/M3 spread +$7.66 — slightly steeper than Dubai. Live spot prints from April: IOC bought from Mercuria at Dated Brent +$17, IOC bought from Total at Dated Brent +$25. Wide premiums to Brent reflect Asian appetite for light sour.

grades.name: ESPO Blend
grades.context: Eastern Siberia-Pacific Ocean blend, delivered Kozmino. ~35° API, low sulfur. Currently $22/bbl below Dated Brent — the cleanest signal of Russia sanction enforcement intensity. Discount has narrowed slightly during this rally as China teapots and Indian state refiners step in. Note: the discount STAYED wide while Brent ripped, meaning sanctions enforcement still effective.

grades.name: Basrah Medium
grades.context: SOMO-marketed, ~30° API, ~2.7% sulfur. Iraq is OPEC's #2 producer. Currently $14/bbl below Brent reflecting medium-sour discount. Live April spot: PVOil bought 2,000 kbbl from unknown seller at OSP -$18 — typical Iraqi term deal pricing. Basrah Heavy printed $107.79 (-$1.50 to Medium).
